mission.title: Install and Configure Jaeger on Kubernetes
mission.description: Jaeger is a distributed tracing platform that helps developers monitor and troubleshoot complex microservices architectures. Installing Jaeger allows you to visualize and analyze the performance of your applications, making it easier to identify bottlenecks and optimize your services.

steps.title: Add the Jaeger Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Jaeger Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add jaegertracing https://jaegertracing.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Jaeger using Helm
steps.description:
Install Jaeger in a new namespace called 'jaeger' with a specific version.
```bash
helm install jaeger jaegertracing/jaeger --namespace jaeger --create-namespace --version 2.15.1
```

steps.title: Verify Jaeger installation
steps.description:
Check that the Jaeger pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace jaeger
```

steps.title: Port forward to access Jaeger UI
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Jaeger UI from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/jaeger-query --namespace jaeger 16686:16686
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration for resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Jaeger deployment to set resource limits for better performance.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment jaeger-collector --namespace jaeger -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"jaeger-collector","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Jaeger will show all pods running in the 'jaeger' namespace. You can access the Jaeger UI at http://localhost:16686 to start monitoring your applications.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add jaegertracing https://jaegertracing.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
